title: platform-user — validation rules
description: Custom Laravel validation rules published by `PlatformUserServiceProvider::boot()`. All four rules are consumed by the HirePlatformUserInput, UpdatePlatformProfileInput, and ReassignManagerInput data classes. Two of them (valid_e164_phone, valid_iana_timezone) mirror rules the tenant-plane user module ships but are re-declared here so the platform-user module has no soft-dependency on `identity/user` beyond the Identity substrate.

rules.name: valid_e164_phone
rules.description: Ensures the value is a valid E.164-formatted phone number. Uses `giggsey/libphonenumber-for-php` under the hood. Required for PlatformProfile.phone_e164 because the on-call escalation resolver dials this number — an invalid value would cause a page to fail silently at 3am. Format: leading `+`, country code, subscriber number (up to 15 digits total). Example: `+14155552671`. Rejects: `(415) 555-2671`, `4155552671`, `+1 415 555 2671` (whitespace).
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid E.164 phone number (leading +, country code, digits only, up to 15 digits total).

rules.name: valid_iana_timezone
rules.description: Ensures the value is a valid IANA timezone identifier (e.g. `America/New_York`, `Europe/London`, `Asia/Tokyo`). Validates against PHP's built-in `DateTimeZone::listIdentifiers()`. Used for PlatformProfile.timezone which drives the on-call rotation's local-time display + shift-handoff computation.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid IANA timezone identifier (e.g. America/New_York).

rules.name: valid_department
rules.description: Ensures the value is a member of the Academorix\PlatformUser\Enums\PlatformDepartment enum. The canonical set is seeded from `data/departments.json` at boot. Validated against a runtime cache so a mistyped department in an admin CLI invocation fails fast with a helpful error listing the valid values.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be one of: ops, product, engineering, support, sales, finance, legal, security, dpo, hr.

rules.name: manager_belongs_to_platform
rules.description: Ensures the value (a PlatformUser id) references an active or suspended PlatformUser (not offboarded, not soft-deleted). Rejects self-references (a PlatformUser cannot manage themselves) unless the `allow_self` argument is passed — that flag exists for the top-of-tree seeding case where the founder / CEO has themselves as their `manager_id` placeholder before the org chart is initialised, and is NEVER used in normal operation.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must reference an active or suspended platform user (not offboarded, not soft-deleted).
additional_checks: Cycle detection: refuse when the proposed manager's chain of managers eventually points back to the current row. Prevents infinite-loop resolution in the org-chart accessor.
Depth cap: refuse when the resulting chain would exceed `platform-user.org_chart.max_depth` (default 8 levels). Prevents pathological org-chart shapes.

usage_from_data_class: The HirePlatformUserInput, UpdatePlatformProfileInput, and ReassignManagerInput data classes auto-emit the FormRequest rule sets. Custom validation errors surface in the standard `data.errors` map on 422 responses; the CLI commands print them one-per-line with the failed field prefixed.
